681fa16d196ad6fc88d8060e.question: After the release of this album, Celia Zaentz mailed a copy of it to Langston Hughes after he had written glowingly in the Chicago Defender about the artist’s Newport Jazz Festival set. Julio de Diego, the father-in-law of its artist’s manager Morris Eagle, designed this album’s surrealist cover. This album was released the year after Atlantic began recording jazz under Nesuhi (nuh-SOO-he) Ertegun. Jackie McLean’s bar fight with this album’s artist and his subsequent arrest for heroin possession caused him to be banned from the (*) Jazz Workshop. The long jams performed by this album’s artist at the Café Bohemia inspired its final track “Love Chant,” while musique concrète inspired its cover of “A Foggy Day.” For 10 points, name this 1956 debut album by Charles Mingus, titled for the former scientific name of Java Man.
681fa16d196ad6fc88d8060e.question_sanitized: After the release of this album, Celia Zaentz mailed a copy of it to Langston Hughes after he had written glowingly in the Chicago Defender about the artist's Newport Jazz Festival set. Julio de Diego, the father-in-law of its artist's manager Morris Eagle, designed this album's surrealist cover. This album was released the year after Atlantic began recording jazz under Nesuhi (nuh-SOO-he) Ertegun. Jackie McLean's bar fight with this album's artist and his subsequent arrest for heroin possession caused him to be banned from the (*) Jazz Workshop. The long jams performed by this album's artist at the Cafe Bohemia inspired its final track "Love Chant," while musique concrete inspired its cover of "A Foggy Day." For 10 points, name this 1956 debut album by Charles Mingus, titled for the former scientific name of Java Man.

681fa170196ad6fc88d80643.question: Eva Cassidy’s liberal interpretation of this standard at a concert in Glendale, Maryland was a posthumous hit in the UK. Sonny Rollins based his composition “Valse Hot” off this standard. Ella Fitzgerald’s version of this song begins with its oft-omitted introduction, which describes a “magic lane” that opens when “all the world is a hopeless jumble.” Louis Mayer wanted to cut this song from the film it appeared in because it was sung in a barnyard. This ballad by (*) Harold Arlen and Yip Harburg was mashed-up with “What a Wonderful World” in a recording by ukulelist Israel Kamakawiwoʻole. A place where “troubles melt like lemon drops” is described in, for 10 points, which jazz standard originated by Judy Garland in The Wizard of Oz?
